description: Stage 3.4: shared-trunk late branching. Train one common trunk, then spend the remaining wallclock on multiple short late finishers and export the best deployed branch.

sanity.description: Crash check and early branch-trigger verification.

screen.description: Short branch-finishers screen on deployed score.

decision.description: Long single-GPU runs for promoted branch strategies.

champion_8x.description: Full-box confirmation run for the best branch strategy.

slots.name: static_control_a
slots.why: Primary static control. No late branching.
slots.validates: The strong static base remains the reference point for branch-stage deltas.
slots.falsifies: All branch comparisons are suspect if control is unstable.

slots.name: static_control_b
slots.why: Noise-floor control with a different seed.
slots.validates: Short-run variance is low enough to judge branch candidates.
slots.falsifies: If controls spread too much, branch-stage deltas are not separable.

slots.name: tri_branch_default
slots.mechanism: Train a shared trunk until 74% of wallclock, then split the remaining budget evenly across three finishers: EMA-heavy, deploy-aligned QAT, and family-split warmdown. Export the best deployed branch.
slots.why: The dominant uncertainty is in late consolidation, not early representation. Shared-trunk branching lets us test multiple late stories without paying the full early-training cost multiple times.
slots.early_signal: Branch trigger log appears on time, all three finishers run, and the winning branch is not trivially the default EMA path.
slots.failure_mode: Each branch gets too little late budget, so none of the finishers improve enough to justify splitting.
slots.kill_rule: No deployed improvement vs control by screen, or the chosen branch is always the trivial EMA finisher.
slots.validates: Shared-trunk late branching is better than one committed late path.
slots.falsifies: Late branching wastes too much budget and one late trajectory is still superior.

slots.name: earlier_tri_branch
slots.broken_invariant: The late-branch window should be tiny.
slots.mechanism: Same three finisher families as H401, but branch earlier at 68% to give each finisher more continuation budget.
slots.why: If late branching is real but underpowered, the failure mode is not branching itself but insufficient finisher time.
slots.early_signal: Winning branch should separate more clearly than H401 if branch-time starvation was the main issue.
slots.failure_mode: Branching too early weakens the common trunk and hurts all finishers.
slots.kill_rule: Worse than H401 and control by screen, or trunk quality visibly collapses.
slots.validates: Earlier branching gives finishers enough time to matter.
slots.falsifies: The shared trunk must stay intact for longer; earlier branch is harmful.

slots.name: late_dual_branch
slots.mechanism: Branch later at 80% and only test two finishers: EMA-heavy and deploy-aligned QAT, giving each branch more budget.
slots.why: The main trade is breadth versus depth. Two stronger finishers may beat three shallow ones.
slots.early_signal: One of the two finishers should win decisively if branch depth matters more than finisher count.
slots.failure_mode: The omitted family-split finisher was the one that mattered, or branching later leaves too little time overall.
slots.kill_rule: No score gain vs control and no gain vs H401/H402.
slots.validates: Finisher budget per branch matters more than branching breadth.
slots.falsifies: Late-branch depth is not the bottleneck.

slots.name: deploy_vs_family_duel
slots.broken_invariant: EMA is the only reasonable late baseline finisher.
slots.mechanism: Branch at 76% and directly duel a deploy-aligned QAT finisher against a family-split finisher, dropping the EMA branch entirely.
slots.why: If the purpose of the branch stage is to test differentiated late mechanisms, keeping a trivial EMA path may waste budget.
slots.early_signal: One of the mechanism-heavy finishers should win; if neither does, the branch stage is not adding value.
slots.failure_mode: Removing EMA makes the whole branch set less robust and both mechanism-heavy finishers underperform.
slots.kill_rule: Both finishers lose to control or the winner is weaker than H401.
slots.validates: Mechanism-heavy late branches are the real value, not the trivial EMA path.
slots.falsifies: EMA remains the dominant late fallback and should stay in the finisher set.

slots.name: raw_ema_deploy_triple
slots.broken_invariant: The only late uncertainty is finisher mechanism, not export state style.
slots.mechanism: Branch at 75% into three style-separated finishers: raw late continuation, EMA-heavy continuation, and deploy-aligned continuation.
slots.why: This isolates whether the gain is coming from export-state style, not just from late policy mechanics.
slots.early_signal: Winning branch identity should tell us whether state style or policy style dominates late performance.
slots.failure_mode: The branches are too similar and the budget split just adds noise.
slots.kill_rule: No branch separates from control or the chosen branch is unstable across horizons.
slots.validates: Late export-state style is a first-order branching dimension.
slots.falsifies: Policy mechanics dominate and state style is secondary.

slots.name: aggressive_tri_branch
slots.broken_invariant: Late finisher policies should stay mild to be safe.
slots.mechanism: Use the same tri-branch structure as H401 but with a more aggressive deploy branch and a stronger family-split branch.
slots.why: If branching works, it gives permission to swing harder in the finishers because the run can discard bad branches afterward.
slots.early_signal: Winning branch should not be the mild EMA path if branching really enables harder late swings.
slots.failure_mode: All aggressive finishers destabilize and the branch stage just falls back to trivial behavior.
slots.kill_rule: No branch beats control or branch winner is always the conservative EMA finisher.
slots.validates: Branching enables harder late finisher policies than a single committed run would tolerate.
slots.falsifies: Even with branching, mild finishers remain superior.
